summary: Soundboard source with poor bass and harsh highs, but the concert shines thanks to the rare debut of “Reuben & Cherise” and solid performances throughout.
review: Fans agree this show is a memorable one – highlighted by the first‑ever Dead rendition of “Reuben & Cherise,” plus tight versions of “Let It Grow,” the CC→IKYR segue, and “The Wheel.”  The recording, however, suffers from severe high‑frequency emphasis, missing low end, hiss and click noise, making it a marginal soundboard copy despite the venue’s excellent on‑stage mix.

ai_rating.rationale: The performance is widely praised (≈4 / 5) while the audio quality is consistently rated poorly (≈2 / 5); weighted combination yields a mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: High‑end strident, almost no bass, noticeable hiss, occasional clicks and possible azimuth/noise‑reduction errors. Multiple reviewers note the tape sounds marginal compared to other known copies.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Unusual set list featuring the rare debut of “Reuben & Cherise” and a relaxed, enjoyable vibe.  Even with Phil’s bass disappearing for parts, the band sounds like they’re having fun.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for his songwriting; reviewers call “Reuben & Cherise” a beautiful ballad and highlight his vocal/guitar work.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted as completely missing in the mix during parts of the first set, making the bass inaudible.
band_member_comments.Bob: His guitar is mentioned as having issues in this particular recording (clicks/feedback).
